name: Dataset Curation and Governance
tagline: Provenance, filtering, and licensing discipline for training data
problem:
Training data determines model behavior. Uncurated corpora contain duplicates, low-quality text, toxic content, private information, and licensing problems.

Dataset curation solves the governance problem: make data selection auditable, repeatable, and safe enough to support long-term systems without legal or ethical collapse.

The remaining problem for Dataset Curation and Governance is operational reliability. A reader needs more than a name and a historical note: they need to know what failure this capability prevents, what resources it consumes, what signs show that it is failing, and when it is safer to stop than to improvise. This entry therefore treats dataset curation and governance as a teachable system with inputs, inspection points, records, and limits rather than as a single trick.
overview:
Curation includes provenance tracking, deduplication, filtering, normalization, and documentation. Governance includes access control, privacy review, and a change log so future teams can reproduce or revise decisions.

For off-world settlement and collapse recovery, curated datasets matter even more: you cannot afford wasted compute or unsafe behavior from corrupted corpora.

In collapse recovery, dataset curation and governance must be repairable with local materials, teachable to more than one person, and documented well enough that the method survives injury, migration, or leadership change. In a space-settlement context, the same capability must account for closed-loop resources, contamination control, spare-part scarcity, power budgets, and the cost of mistakes in a sealed habitat. The practical minimum is a controlled trial, written checks, safe storage, and review before scale-up.

principles.name: Provenance
principles.explanation: Record where data came from and under what conditions it can be used.

principles.name: Deduplication
principles.explanation: Reduce memorization and overcounting; improves training efficiency and evaluation integrity.

principles.name: Risk management
principles.explanation: Filter unsafe content; protect privacy; document known gaps and biases.

components.name: Use-case boundary
components.function: Defines what Dataset Curation and Governance is meant to accomplish and what it must not be used for
components.madeFrom: Written requirements, hazard notes, local constraints, and responsible operator assignment
components.criticalNote: A vague use case leads to unsafe improvisation.

rawMaterials.name: Electric power
rawMaterials.purpose: Energy for computation, storage, and networking
earthLocations: Electrical grids globally; generators in remote sites
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Solar + batteries is the simplest early-space pathway; plan for tight energy budgets and thermal rejection.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Stable voltage regulation, grounding, and basic power-quality control.

rawMaterials.name: Semiconductor devices
rawMaterials.purpose: Transistors and ICs that perform switching, storage, and signal conditioning
earthLocations: Global semiconductor supply chains; packaged IC distributors
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Early colonies import packaged devices; long-term local manufacture requires clean chemistry and crystal growth capabilities.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: ESD-safe handling; correct packaging, heat sinking, and derating for temperature and radiation.

rawMaterials.name: Documentation and standards
rawMaterials.purpose: Make designs reproducible and debuggable across teams and time
earthLocations: Libraries, universities, standards bodies, archived web
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Mirror core references offline; maintain printed and digital copies with checksums and redundancy.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Versioning, change control, and clear measurement units and assumptions.

rawMaterials.name: Raw corpora
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Local archives and manuals; prioritize high-signal technical references and vetted local logs.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Parsing, deduplication, filtering, and metadata extraction.

buildSteps.title: Define data policy
buildSteps.description: Specify what content is allowed, how provenance is tracked, and how privacy is protected.
buildSteps.warningNote: No policy means accidental data misuse.

buildSteps.title: Ingest with metadata
buildSteps.description: Store raw data with hashes, source identifiers, and licensing notes.
buildSteps.warningNote: If you cannot trace origin, you cannot debug model behavior.

buildSteps.title: Deduplicate and filter
buildSteps.description: Remove near-duplicates; filter low-quality or unsafe content; record filter rules.
buildSteps.warningNote: Silent filters create hidden bias; document changes.

buildSteps.title: Version and audit
buildSteps.description: Publish dataset versions with changelogs and sample inspections.

buildSteps.title: Define the local purpose and stop conditions
buildSteps.description: Write down why Dataset Curation and Governance is needed, who is responsible, what success looks like, and which conditions require stopping or escalation.
buildSteps.warningNote: Do not start a life-critical or infrastructure-critical use without clear stop conditions.

history.event: Data governance grows with web-scale systems.
Large-scale datasets become central to deep learning.
Dataset transparency and safety become critical concerns.

impact:
Dataset governance determines whether AI systems are reproducible and safe. It reduces wasted compute and prevents preventable harms caused by contaminated corpora.

In The Codex, dataset curation is part of the 'how to build AI' chain: models are downstream of data decisions.
